leadText: This article is about the move Defog. For the field move Demist, see Field Move. Defog (Japanese: きりばらい Fog Wipe) is a non-damaging Flying-type move introduced in Generation IV. It was HM05 in Diamond, Pearl, and Platinum, and it was TM97 in Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl. Defog cannot be used outside of battle without the Fen Badge in Diamond and Pearl and the Relic Badge in Platinum.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generation IV 1.1.2 Generation V 1.1.3 Generations VI and VII 1.1.4 Generation VIII onwards 1.2 Outside of battle 1.2.1 Sinnoh based games 1.2.2 HeartGold and SoulSilver to Sword and Shield, and Generation IX Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation IV [ edit | edit source ] Defog lowers the target's evasion one stage, even if the target is behind a substitute . It bypasses accuracy checks to always hit, unless the target is in the semi-invulnerable turn of a move such as Dig or Fly . Defog clears away any fog on the field, as well as Light Screen , Reflect , Safeguard , Mist , Spikes , Toxic Spikes , and Stealth Rock on the target's side of the field. Generation V [ edit | edit source ] Defog can now be reflected with Magic Coat and no longer lowers the evasion of a Pokémon behind a substitute . Generations VI and VII [ edit | edit source ] Defog now clears away Spikes , Toxic Spikes , Stealth Rock , and Sticky Web on both the user's and target's side of the field. In addition to Light Screen, Reflect, Safeguard, and Mist, it also clears away Aurora Veil from the target's side of the field only. If powered up by a Flyinium Z into Z-Defog, the user's accuracy is raised by one stage. Generation VIII onwards [ edit | edit source ] Defog now also clears terrains and the effects of G-Max Steelsurge . Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] In certain games, Defog requires a specific badge to use outside of battle; the Fen Badge D P BD SP or Relic Badge Pt . Sinnoh based games [ edit | edit source ] Defog clears away fog from the area. The effect lasts until the player leaves the area. HeartGold and SoulSilver to Sword and Shield, and Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Defog can no longer be used outside of battle.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description D P Pt PBR Obstacles are moved, reducing the foe's evasion stat. It can also be used to clear deep fog, etc. HG SS It reduces the foe's evasion stat. It also removes spikes, light screens, etc. B W B2 W2 A strong wind blows away the target's obstacles such as Reflect or Light Screen. It also lowers the target's evasiveness. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh LA S V A strong wind blows away the target's barriers such as Reflect or Light Screen. This also lowers the target's evasiveness. BD SP A strong wind blows away the target’s barriers, such as Reflect or Light Screen, and lowers their evasiveness. It’s also one of the Pokétch’s hidden moves.
